title: Specialist medical practitioners and consultants
description: Conduct research in the genetics, nutrition, reproduction, growth, and development of domestic farm animals. Use chemistry, microbiology, engineering, and other sciences to study the principles underlying the processing and deterioration of foods; analyze food content to determine levels of vitamins, fat, sugar, and protein; discover new food sources; research ways to make processed foods safe, palatable, and healthful; and apply food science knowledge to determine best ways to process, package, preserve, store, and distribute food. Conduct research in breeding, physiology, production, yield, and management of crops and agricultural plants or trees, shrubs, and nursery stock, their growth in soils, and control of pests; or study the chemical, physical, biological, and mineralogical composition of soils as they relate to plant or crop growth. May classify and map soils and investigate effects of alternative practices on soil and crop productivity. (Based on 52 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Examine patients to diagnose oral health conditions and diseases, and assess abnormalities of jaw development and related dental structures.
Determine brain death using accepted tests and procedures.
Develop treatment plans based on diagnoses, evaluations of factors such as age, general health, and procedural risks or costs.
Diagnose neurological conditions based on interpretation of examination findings, histories, or test results.
Inform patients or families of neurological diagnoses, prognoses, and the benefits, risks, and costs of treatment plans.
Interview patients to obtain information, such as complaints, symptoms, and medical and family histories.
Order supportive care services, such as physical therapy and specialized nursing care.
Manage medical laboratories and direct, coordinate, or supervise the patient care activities of nursing or support staff.
Develop or adopt new tests or instruments to improve the diagnosis of diseases and assess their clinical significance.
Counsel patients or others on the background of neurological disorders including risk factors and genetic or environmental concerns.
Write patient discharge summaries and send them to primary care physicians, including documenting all procedures performed and their outcomes.
Participate in neuroscience research activities and communicate findings through presentations or reports.
Prescribe medications, treatment regimens, or therapies based on patient evaluations and monitor for side effects or complications.
Collaborate with patients' primary care physicians and other healthcare practitioners to maintain continuity and quality of care.
Train or supervise medical students, residents, or other health professionals, and engage in continuing education activities to maintain expertise.
Order or interpret results of tests such as laboratory tests and neuroimaging studies like MRI and SPECT scans.
Develop and monitor procedures to ensure adequate quality control of imaging or laboratory services.
Attend inpatient consultations in areas of specialty, and conduct primary assessments that include information from prior medical care.
Select and prepare medical equipment or medications to be taken to athletic competition sites.
Evaluate and manage chronic pain conditions, diagnose or treat disorders of the musculoskeletal system, and examine, evaluate and treat athletes who have been injured.
Examine and evaluate athletes prior to participation in sports activities to determine their level of fitness or risk of injury.
Develop and test procedures for dealing with emergencies during practices or competitions, and coordinate sports care activities with experts.
Conduct research to develop or test medications, treatments, or procedures that prevent or control disease or injury; and engage in research activities related to medical genetics or counseling.
Advise patients and community members concerning diet, activity, hygiene, or disease prevention, and provide counseling to patients and families.
Monitor patients' conditions and progress, and evaluate treatment outcomes.
Document or review patients' medical histories and prepare consultation reports to provide information on complex medical concepts.
Discuss testing options, associated risks, and benefits with patients and families to assist in informed decision-making.
Order and interpret the results of diagnostic tests, consult with or provide consulting services to other physicians, and refer patients to specialists when necessary.
Teach, train medical staff, and provide educational materials related to genetics or medical topics for patients and medical personnel.
Collaborate with specialists and refer clients to services that enhance functioning when conditions exceed scope of practice.
Collaborate with others to design or implement interdisciplinary treatment programs.
Apply selected research findings to practice and conduct music therapy research.
Observe, analyze, and document patients' participation and progress during treatment sessions, modifying programs as needed.
Develop treatment plans to meet the needs of patients based on assessments and patient interests.
Evaluate patients' progress and prepare reports detailing treatment outcomes and effectiveness.
Counsel patients to develop leisure activities and improve decision making and life-management skills.
Plan and implement programs and activities to help patients learn work or school skills and adjust to disabilities.
Communicate with clients to build rapport and acknowledge their progress during therapy sessions.
Maintain necessary records and document treatment plans, case summaries, and evaluations related to clients.
Instruct groups in therapy techniques and educate the public about the benefits of therapeutic practices.
Diagnose health conditions and provide nonsurgical treatment plans based on patients' symptoms and health histories.
Consult with other health professionals to provide optimal patient care and manage treatment plans.
Instruct medical and graduate students in methods or procedures for diagnosis and management of genetic disorders.
Supervise biological technicians and technologists, postdoctoral research fellows, and other scientists.
Provide scientific direction for project teams regarding the evaluation or handling of devices, drugs, or cells for disease models.
Conduct research on genetic composition of animal populations and heritability of traits using principles of genetics.
Investigate diseases or parasites to determine cause, risk factors, and transmission modes.
Plan, administer, and evaluate health safety standards, consulting with health professionals to improve public health.
Consult with physicians, educators, researchers, and government health officials regarding medical applications of biological sciences.
Write grant proposals and applications to obtain funding for biological and medical research.
Prepare reports and communicate research findings to the scientific community and the public.
Conduct research to develop methodologies and procedures for medical application, analyzing data and presenting findings.
Study nutritional requirements and effects of management practices on animal products to improve quality and quantity.
Conduct research on animal nutrition, breeding, and management practices to enhance production efficiency and quality.
Develop improved practices for feeding, housing, sanitation, and disease control of animals.
Create and modify bioinformatics tools and applications for analyzing biological data effectively.
Evaluate food processing and storage operations to ensure compliance with regulations and develop quality assurance programs.
Develop new or improved ways of preserving and processing foods, using knowledge of chemistry and microbiology.
Study the structure, composition, and changes of food during processing to improve safety and nutritional value.
Conduct applied research for improvements in disease testing, crop quality, and pharmaceuticals.
Develop guidelines for managing biological processes and improving public health standards.
Maintain laboratory safety programs and train personnel in safety techniques.
Analyze data related to foodborne diseases and environmental health to identify public health issues.
Design and apply bioinformatics algorithms including unsupervised and supervised machine learning or graphic algorithms.
Create novel computational approaches and analytical tools as required by research goals.
Compile data for use in activities like gene expression profiling, genome annotation, or structural bioinformatics.
Communicate research results through conference presentations, publications, or project reports.
Consult with researchers to analyze problems and recommend technology-based solutions or computational strategies.
Analyze large molecular datasets, such as raw microarray, genomic sequence, or proteomics data, for clinical or basic research.
Conduct research to develop methodologies, instrumentation, and procedures for medical application and present findings.
Develop new products and procedures for sterilization, food preservation, or microbial contamination detection.
Monitor and perform tests on water, food, and the environment to detect harmful microorganisms or sources of contamination.
Conduct experiments to develop new or improved varieties of field crops, focusing on traits like yield, quality, or disease resistance.
Study ways to improve agricultural sustainability, including new methods of composting.
Develop environmentally safe methods or products for controlling or eliminating weeds, crop diseases, or pests.
Investigate soil problems or poor water quality to determine sources and effects.
Identify degraded or contaminated soils and develop plans to improve their chemical, biological, or physical characteristics.
